rulings.Case Title: GSQUARED RESTAURANT VENTURES, LLC VS. COCOA DEVELOPMENT RETAIL, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 10, 2013, line 5. CROSS DEFENDANT GSQUARED RESTAURANT VENTURES, LLC's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT DENIED. Cross-complaint argues in opposition and is bound by its argument that Cross-complaint does not seek special damages stemming from foreclosure. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: GSQUARED RESTAURANT VENTURES, LLC VS. COCOA DEVELOPMENT RETAIL, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 10, 2013, line 5. CROSS DEFENDANT GSQUARED RESTAURANT VENTURES, LLC's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend to allege facts supporting "bad faith" and to allege breaches/damages separate and distinct form breach of contract. See Careau. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: GSQUARED RESTAURANT VENTURES, LLC VS. COCOA DEVELOPMENT RETAIL, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 10, 2013, line 5. PLAINTIFF GSQUARED RESTAURANT VENTURES, LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Interrogatories GRANTED. Defendant shall have 10 days from the notice of entry of order to provide further responses. No sanctions. Responding party was justified in objecting to the term "INCIDENT" as defined in the Form Interrogatories; the term was sufficiently defined in "meet and confer" letters and this motion. 12.4 and 12.5: Defendant shall identify all photographs, films, videotapes, diagrams, reproductions and models made from November 2007 to February 2011 depicting areas tenant was supposed to be entitled to use under the lease. If such documents were produced in Request for Production they must be identified by bates number or by name; 12.6: Defendant shall identify all reports including informal and privileged reports. If such documents were produced in Request for Production they must be identified by bates number or by name; 14.1: Defendant shall provide further responses based on definition of "INCIDENT" as provided in Plaintiff's Separate Statement 3:14-19. Also, Privilege is not applicable if actual contention is being made. 50.6: Defendant shall respond to this interrogatory. Reading and analyzing a document that forms the basis of this litigation is not unreasonable or overly burdensome. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: PETER GRIMES et al VS. COUNTRY WIDE BANK, AS THE ORIGINAL LENDER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Pendency Of Action
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 10, 2013, line 1. PLAINTIFF PETER GRIMES and AGNES GRIMES' Motion For Pendency Of Action OFF CALENDAR. Moving party failed to comply with SFLR 2.6B and this Court's minute order of August 20, 2013. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: ART WALKER VS. PENNYMAC LOAN SERVICES, LLC et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 10, 2013, line 2. DEFENDANT HAPPY OCEAN, LLC, and POST APPLE, LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Continued to October 30, 2013 to be heard with other pending motions. Opposing party and its counsel is ordered to comply with SFLR 2.6B and provide courtesy copies of the opposition to department 501 no later than September 20, 2013 with cover letter reflecting new hearing date. = (501/HMD)

rulings.Case Title: SOJOURN PROPERTIES, INC. VS. KRYSTYNA K. LOVELL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, September 10, 2013, line 4. DEFENDANT KRYSTYNA LOVELL's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND GRANTED, subject to (1) opposition; (2) filing answer no later that September 10, 2013 by 4 p.m.; and (3) posting $645 with the Court no later than September 10, 2013 by 4 p.m. = (501/HMD)
